description: Uses an instanceof chain to handle different BooleanExpression types, requiring modification for every new expression type.
reasoning: The buildCondition method explicitly checks for every known subtype of BooleanExpression (Eq, RegexEq, In, etc.) using an if-else instanceof chain. Adding a new expression type to the Logstash IR would require modifying this method to add a new branch, which violates the Open/Closed Principle.

description: Switch statement on protocol strings prevents extension without modification.
reasoning: The getPipelineSourceDetails method uses a switch statement to map protocol strings ('string', 'file', etc.) to display names. Adding a new configuration source protocol requires modifying this existing class, rather than allowing the protocol to provide its own description through polymorphism or a registry.

description: Switching on FieldReference types requires modification for new field namespaces.
reasoning: The Event class uses switch statements (in getUnconvertedField, setField, includes, and remove) to handle different types of FieldReferences (META_PARENT, META_CHILD, etc.). If Logstash were to add a new metadata namespace or field type, the core Event class would need to be modified in multiple places to handle the new type.

description: Hardcoded instantiation of MmapPageIOV2 prevents extending the storage mechanism.
reasoning: The Queue class directly instantiates MmapPageIOV2 in openPages and newCheckpointedHeadpage. This tight coupling means that the Queue is not open for extension regarding its underlying PageIO implementation; adding a new storage format or version would require modifying the Queue class itself.

description: Type-checking logic for configuration expansion prevents support for new data types.
reasoning: The expandConfigVariable method uses a series of instanceof checks to determine how to expand configuration values (List, Map, String). Adding a new type of configuration object that requires expansion would necessitate modifying this method.
